dailyJudgementSnippet: Google Gemini won because the only tracked provider with clearly constructive HN discussion centered on Gemma’s local/on-device use: one Show HN drew praise for real-time local assistant viability and fine-tuning flexibility, while another was received as a neat privacy-first browser experiment despite some security caveats [E6][E7].
winnerExplanation: Google Gemini edged the day on breadth and tone. It had two meaningful Gemma-related front-page stories, and the comments were mostly about practical model usefulness in local deployment rather than distrust, regressions, or botched UX. That left it with the strongest net vibe even though the praise was measured and caveated [E6][E7].

openai.judgementSnippet: The day for OpenAI was driven almost entirely by the Sam Altman trust story, where top comments praised the reporting and one prominent comment alleged troubling behavior affecting OpenAI hiring decisions [E1].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Anthropic’s biggest story was a long complaint thread saying Claude Code had become unusable for complex work, with commenters accusing the company of silently reducing thinking depth and describing cancellations; a separate vibe-coding thread still included users defending Claude Code as useful for cleanup and refactors [E2][E3].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Gemma-centered Show HNs gave Google its best signal: commenters liked the prospect of local assistants and noted E2B can be fine-tuned, while an in-browser Gemma demo was seen as neat and privacy-friendly even as some raised security and lifecycle concerns [E6][E7].

microsoft_copilot.judgementSnippet: Copilot mentions were sparse but unfavorable: one commenter said Microsoft squandered Bing/Copilot’s early web-connected chat advantage with poor conversation handling, and another said the same recent Opus degradation was showing up through Copilot too [E4][E5].

evidence.summary: Front-page story about whether Sam Altman can be trusted; top comments called the reporting strong, and one visible comment alleged bias and harmful effects on hiring at OpenAI.

Claude Code issue thread said the product had become unusable for complex engineering after updates, with comments accusing Anthropic of silently nerfing thinking depth and making the subscription worse.

In the vibe-coding discussion, several commenters used Claude Code as an example of a still-useful tool, saying it can produce valuable cleanup/refactor PRs and power a successful product even if the source looks messy.

On the Microsoft GUI strategy thread, a top comment argued Bing/Copilot had an early chance to replace Google-style search with web-enabled AI chat but was hamstrung by blocked multi-turn conversations and poor UX.

A comment in the Claude Code degradation thread said similar worsening Opus behavior was also visible when accessed through Copilot, extending some of the frustration to Microsoft’s wrapper experience.

Show HN for a real-time local assistant built with Gemma E2B prompted comments praising local-model progress, the usefulness of on-device assistants, and Gemma E2B’s fine-tunability, with one caveat that Gemma 4 still feels heavy.

Show HN for an in-browser Gemma app was received as a neat no-cloud idea with privacy appeal, but comments also raised concerns about giving a local model JS privileges and tying inference to browser-tab lifecycle.
